questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Human Development Index (HDI) is a composite index measuring average achievement in three basic dimensions of human development.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The three dimensions include life expectancy, education and income.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: HDI is a composite index and the given dimensions are indeed the ones it measures.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Gross National Income (GNI) is a better indicator of a country's standard of living.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It takes into account the income earned by the citizens of a country, regardless of their location.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: GNI is indeed a better indicator and the given reason is correct.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Human Poverty Index (HPI) is used to measure the deprivation of people from a long, healthy and decent life.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It focuses on those dimensions which are essential for a person's well-being and dignity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: HPI indeed measures deprivation and the given reason is correct.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) is a good indicator of the health status of a country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a reliable indicator of the quality of healthcare and living conditions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: IMR is indeed a good indicator and the given reason is correct.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The total fertility rate (TFR) is a measure of the average number of children a woman would have in her lifetime.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a good indicator of the fertility rate of a population.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: TFR indeed measures the average number of children a woman would have and it's a good indicator of fertility rate.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Gender Development Index (GDI) is an alternative to the Human Development Index (HDI).
questions.reason: Reason (R): It takes into account the gender disparities in health, education and income.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: GDI is an alternative to HDI and the given reason is correct.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Life Expectancy at Birth (LEB) is a measure of the average number of years a person is expected to live.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a good indicator of the health status of a population.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: LEB indeed measures the average number of years a person is expected to live and it's a good indicator of health status.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is a measure of the deprivation of people from multiple aspects of life.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It considers multiple dimensions such as health, education and living standards.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: MPI indeed considers multiple dimensions and the given reason is correct.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Human Development Index (HDI) has three dimensions: life expectancy, education and income.
questions.reason: Reason (R): These dimensions are equally weighted in the calculation of HDI.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: HDI indeed has three dimensions and they are equally weighted.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Gross National Expenditure (GNE) is a better indicator of a country's standard of living than the Gross National Income (GNI).
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a more comprehensive measure of a country's economic well-being.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: GNI is a better indicator than GNE.
